name: cf-plan

criteria.name: completeness
criteria.description: Plan covers all aspects of the task without missing major steps

scoring.0: Plan is a stub with one or two vague bullet points
scoring.1: Plan covers the happy path but misses error handling, edge cases, or cleanup
scoring.2: Plan is mostly complete but omits one non-obvious but important step
scoring.3: Plan covers all steps including setup, implementation, testing, and edge cases

criteria.name: task_granularity
criteria.description: Tasks are broken down to an actionable, implementable level

scoring.0: Tasks are high-level and vague (e.g., 'implement the feature')
scoring.1: Some tasks are actionable but others are too coarse to act on directly
scoring.2: Most tasks are well-scoped but a few could be split further
scoring.3: Every task is specific, scoped to a single change, with target files identified

criteria.name: feasibility
criteria.description: Plan is realistic and implementable within the project's constraints

scoring.0: Plan requires non-existent APIs, impossible architecture, or contradicts project conventions
scoring.1: Plan is technically possible but ignores significant project constraints or conventions
scoring.2: Plan is feasible but underestimates complexity in one area
scoring.3: Plan is realistic, respects project conventions, and accounts for complexity

criteria.name: risk_identification
criteria.description: Plan identifies risks, trade-offs, and alternatives considered

scoring.0: No mention of risks, trade-offs, or alternative approaches
scoring.1: Mentions one risk but no mitigation strategy or alternatives
scoring.2: Identifies key risks with mitigation but misses a significant trade-off
scoring.3: Clearly identifies risks with mitigations and explains why chosen approach is preferred over alternatives

notes: Plans should include a verify step for each task. Look for evidence that the planner explored the codebase before planning (file references, existing pattern awareness).
